leads.name: Carnegie Mellon University Press
leads.title: Series Editor, Poetry and Translation
leads.notes: University press at Carnegie Mellon, Pittsburgh, PA -- 4.5 hrs from Ann Arbor. ~16 lit titles/year. Active spring 2026 list includes poetry (Cameo Blue, Killing Orpheus, Konbit), fiction, and drama. Jake Grefenstette is Series Editor for Poetry + Translation. Strong university press with recent 50-year milestone and new Tayur Endowed Poetry Fund. Distributed by U Chicago Press.

leads.name: Saturnalia Books
leads.title: Managing Editor
leads.notes: Independent poetry press near Philadelphia, PA. ~6-8 titles/year. Publishers Timothy Liu and Sarah Wetzel; Rebecca Lauren is Managing Editor. Known for bold, experimental poetry. CLMP member. Active social/web presence. Recent titles include adventurous voices in contemporary American poetry.

leads.name: White Pine Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: Nonprofit independent literary press in Buffalo, NY -- only 175 miles from Ann Arbor, the closest major proximity lead in this run. Founded 1973. ~6-8 titles/year. Poetry, fiction, translation. Annual White Pine Press Poetry Prize (31st competition in 2026, winner Elizabeth Chapman's Our Long Float). Director Dennis Maloney has run press for decades.

leads.name: Press 53
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Independent literary press in Winston-Salem, NC since 2005. ~10-15 titles/year of poetry and short fiction. Over 300 titles, 100+ awards. Tom Lombardo is Poetry Editor, Kevin Morgan Watson is founder/publisher. Active contest program (2027 Award for Poetry now open). Solid web/social presence. Well-regarded in small press community.

leads.name: Action Books
leads.title: Managing Editor
leads.notes: Independent press at the University of Notre Dame, publishing form-breaking poetry and translation since 2005. ~4-6 titles/year. Editors Johannes Goransson and Joyelle McSweeney; Katherine Hedeen is Managing Editor (Professor of Spanish at Kenyon College, Gambier, OH). Notre Dame is only 2.5 hrs from Ann Arbor. Three books on Best Translated Book Award poetry shortlist. Active spring 2025/2026 release schedule (April 2025 release party at Notre Dame).

leads.name: Lost Horse Press
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Independent poetry press in Sandpoint, Idaho since 1998. ~5-6 titles/year. 130+ books of poetry. Grace Mahoney is new publisher (founder Christine Holbert now Publisher Emerita). Annual Idaho Prize for Poetry. New Regenerations: Indigenous Poetry Series launched recently. Active web presence. Grace Mahoney has a PhD in Ukrainian/Slavic Studies from University of Michigan -- Michigan connection is a natural talking point.

leads.name: Anhinga Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry press in Tallahassee, FL since 1974. ~4-6 titles/year including full collections and chapbooks. Director Rick Campbell (also a poet). Active 2025-2026 with Pushcart nominations, 2025 Rick Campbell Chapbook Prize, open reading period results announced (two manuscripts selected). Contact is the general press email info@anhinga.org, as no separate director email is published on site -- flagged accordingly.

leads.name: Elixir Press
leads.title: Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press in Denver/Westminster, CO since 2000. ~4-6 titles/year of poetry and literary fiction. Two poetry contests and one fiction contest annually. Founder/Editor Dana Curtis. CLMP member. Email info@elixirpress.com is the official press contact (Dana Curtis listed as sole operator). Press is currently not accepting unsolicited submissions, but contest-based model is active.

leads.name: YesYes Books
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Independent poetry press in Portland, OR since 2011. ~6-8 titles/year of provocative contemporary poetry. Publisher KMA Sullivan, Managing Editor Jill Kolongowski. Strong reputation for bold, diverse voices. CLMP member. Active social/web presence. Known for experimental and formally innovative collections.

leads.name: Marsh Hawk Press
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Independent nonprofit poetry press in East Rockaway, NY since 2001. 6-10 titles/year including poetry and poetic memoirs. Publisher Sandy McIntosh, Contributing Editor Tony Trigilio. 150+ titles in print. Active 2026 Marsh Hawk Press Poetry Prizes open. Known for connecting poetry with memoir and visual arts. CLMP member.

leads.name: Wesleyan University Press
leads.title: Assistant Editor, Poetry
leads.notes: University press at Wesleyan University, Middletown, CT. ~25 titles/year across poetry, music, dance, film. Oliver Egger is Assistant Editor focusing on poetry acquisitions. Director Suzanna Tamminen. Cardinal Poetry Prize active (open through Feb 2026). Active poetry list. 2025 Publishers Weekly Higher Learning feature. Borderline on title count but within <25 threshold.

leads.name: Barrow Street Press
leads.title: Senior Associate Editor
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press in New York since 1994. 6-8 titles/year. Annual poetry book prize (2026 contest open, judge Srikanth Reddy; 2025 winner Pagan Era by Phillip Schaefer, chosen by John Murillo). Recently added prose book contest. Rachel Rothenberg is Senior Associate Editor. Strong contest track record and CLMP member. Active submission/contest cycle.

leads.name: Bull City Press
leads.title: Founder and Executive Director
leads.notes: Independent micropress in Durham, NC since 2006. ~10-15 titles/year including full-length collections, chapbooks, and the quarterly magazine Inch. Publishes Frost Place Chapbook Competition winner annually. Founder/Executive Director Ross White (own poetry collection published 2025). Active social presence, recent CLMP member. Chapbook and short-run focus makes them ideal Cushing-Malloy candidate.

leads.name: Broadstone Books
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Independent literary press in Frankfort, KY (approx 420 miles from Ann Arbor, within proximity bonus range) since 2004. ~10-15 titles/year of national poetry. Publisher Larry W. Moore, Editor-in-Chief Sheila, partner Stephanie Potter. Over 220 books by 180+ authors in 22 years. Active 2025-2026 catalog. Well-established, award-winning press. Frankfort, KY is approx 6 hrs from Ann Arbor.

leads.name: Ashland Poetry Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: University-affiliated poetry press at Ashland University, Ashland, OH -- only 155 miles from Ann Arbor. Founded 1969. ~4-6 titles/year. Director Chuck Carlise, Managing Editor Deja Jones. Annual Richard Snyder Prize (2025 winner K.T. Landon's Abide, selected by Kim Addonizio). Undergraduate literary journal Passages: Revised. Distributor: University of Chicago Press. Active 2025 yearbook published.

leads.name: Bottom Dog Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: Nonprofit independent literary press in Huron, OH -- only 100 miles from Ann Arbor, the closest lead by distance in this run. Founded 1985, 35+ years, 230+ books. Director Larry Smith. Five series: Midwest, Working Lives, Harmony, Memoirs, and Appalachian Writing. ~6-8 titles/year. Focus on working-class and Appalachian literature -- a distinct niche. Email Lsmithdog@smithdocs.net is Larry Smith's direct contact.

leads.name: Jacar Press
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Community-active literary press in Durham, NC since approximately 2010. Publisher Richard Krawiec. ~6-10 titles/year including full collections, chapbooks, anthologies, and limited-edition letterpress art books. Explicit mission to use proceeds for progressive community organizations. Multiple formats require diverse manufacturing capabilities. Active 2025-2026 per NC Writers Network.

leads.name: Main Street Rag Publishing
leads.title: Publisher and Managing Editor
leads.notes: Literary publisher relocated from Charlotte, NC to Edinboro, PA in early 2025. ~10-15 titles/year including poetry collections, chapbooks, and magazine issues. Publisher/Managing Editor M. Scott Douglass. Annual MSR Poetry Award for full-length collections and chapbook contest. Magazine running continuously since 1996. Edinboro, PA is approximately 260 miles from Ann Arbor (within proximity bonus).

leads.name: Lily Poetry Review Books
leads.title: Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: Active independent poetry press affiliated with Lily Poetry Review journal, based in Massachusetts. Editor-in-Chief Eileen Cleary (nurse and poet). ~10-15 titles/year in 2025-2026 (highly active: 8+ spring 2026 releases alone visible on website). Annual Paul Nemser Book Prize. Also runs chapbooks and Lily Poetry Salon retreat. One of the most prolific small poetry presses currently operating based on catalog volume.

leads.name: Texas Review Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: University press at Sam Houston State University in Huntsville, TX since 1979. ~10-15 titles/year of poetry, fiction, creative nonfiction, and scholarship. Director J. Bruce Fuller, Managing Editor Pete Carlisle. AUPresses member, CLMP member, Texas A&M Book Consortium. National distribution. Active contest program. Known as 'TRP: The University Press of SHSU' since rebranding.
